expectations.evidence: plays `c` three times within the same time slot that one `c` would normally occupy. Each repetition is shorter/faster — the total duration stays the same, but the note plays 3× faster.

expectations.text: Explains the ! operator (replicate at normal speed)
expectations.evidence: adds three `c` slots at normal speed — exactly like writing `c c c`. Each event keeps its full original duration.

expectations.text: Makes clear that * is faster while ! keeps same tempo
expectations.evidence: `*` divides the slot (makes each event faster); `!` duplicates without changing speed (adds more equal-length slots).

expectations.text: Includes code examples showing both * and ! usage
expectations.evidence: note("c*3") // c plays 3 times, each is 1/3 the duration ... note("c!3") // c c c — three full-length events
